page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-8-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 63.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 7.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include GPU resources, and local storage capacity is designated as None.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4d-highmem-8-lssd offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. This density allows for efficient handling of memory-bound workloads without paying for unnecessary compute cores. However, the lack of integrated GPU accelerators and local NVMe storage limits its utility for tasks requiring high-speed local caching or hardware-accelerated parallel processing.

This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute power.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-8-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 63.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU, positioning it for memory-intensive operations. The instance does not include GPU resources, and local storage is listed as None. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server allows organizations to run memory-bound applications without paying for excess compute cores. However, the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration limits its suitability for high-speed local caching or hardware-accelerated parallel processing. The c4d-highmem-8-lssd is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring a dense memory footprint relative to processing power.
